title: agrostis castellana subsp. mutica
intro: Agrostis castellana subsp. mutica, also known as the Spanish bent or Spanish grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are believed to be attributed to its rich content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Agrostis castellana subsp. mutica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Agrostis castellana subsp. mutica can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by mixing the plant material with a solvent like ethanol.
